hero.title: Joshua Hong
hero.metaLine: 2025–26 Freshman Season | UTSA Men's Golf | NCAA Division I
hero.definition: High-floor freshman scorer with verified Division I production, low round-to-round volatility, and room to grow into a higher team role.
hero.supporting: A completed freshman-season sample showing steady team-score contribution, controlled scoring range, and documented low-round capability.

quickSummary: Recorded a 71.20 Clippd-adjusted average and set UTSA’s single-season raw scoring record at 71.73 over 33 rounds.
32 of 33 team rounds counted, with a 64–76 scoring range across the completed freshman season.
Season production included one individual win, three top-3 finishes, five top-10s, and contribution to a team win.
Scoring ceiling was documented by a 64 (-8), a 134 (-10), and six sub-70 rounds.

stages.title: Junior-to-College Scoring Translation
stages.desc: Joshua’s first college season showed clear junior-to-NCAA scoring translation through steady team contribution, low-round capability, and a completed 33-round sample.

stages.title: Back-to-Back Mexico Season Champion
stages.desc: Six consecutive wins and a second straight Mexico season title reinforced finishing ability, cumulative control, and long-form scoring value.

stages.title: Season Champion Before the Wins
stages.desc: The early profile separated through consistency, stability, and score accumulation before the later win volume arrived.

points: Individual champion and only player under par in the field.
Won at TPC San Antonio in a field where par held clear scoring value.
Contributed to UTSA’s team title in the same event.
Held the team’s highest Clippd weighted contribution across the completed freshman season.
Counted in 32 of 33 team rounds as a freshman.
Freshman scoring profile remained contained across a completed 33-round sample.

evidenceProjection.projectedRoleBody: Lineup-ready profile with team-score stability, scoring-range control, and value in internal lineup competition.
evidenceProjection.translationText: Reliable counting-score profile with low-round evidence and controlled round-to-round scoring risk.
evidenceProjection.developmentText: Freshman production, scoring progression, and low-round evidence support continued role expansion as the college schedule broadens.

cultureFit: Works within team structure and daily practice expectations.
Maintains steady preparation habits across tournament weeks.
Responsive to coaching feedback and technical adjustment.
Handled meaningful team scoring responsibility as a freshman.
Reliable teammate profile with consistent competitive preparation.

transferObjective.body: Evaluating a next-step program fit focused on continued player development, expanded competitive exposure, and long-term college golf progression, while carrying forward the competitive foundation and team experience gained during his freshman year at UTSA.
transferObjective.noteText: Grateful for the UTSA freshman-year opportunity to compete, adapt, learn, and contribute within the program; the next-step review is framed around fit, timing, and long-term development path while respecting the foundation built at UTSA.
